svg.text: • Dense graphs with adjacency-matrix input — O(V²) array Prim beats sort-based Kruskal.
• When you only need the MST starting from a specific root (e.g., broadcast tree).
• Embedded / memory-constrained: O(V) state vs Kruskal's O(V + E) sort buffer.
• Implicit graphs where adjacency is computed on demand (geometric MST, k-NN graphs).
• When edges have variable cost models — recompute on access without sort restart.
• Heap-based pipeline already in place (e.g., Dijkstra infrastructure).
• Animated visualization — frontier growth is intuitive to display.
• Broadcast trees: minimum-cost dissemination from a root in mesh networks.
• Computer graphics: mesh simplification, point-cloud connectivity, depth-image surfacing.
• Image quilting / texture synthesis: minimum-error boundary cut via MST on grid.
• Approximation for traveling salesman: MST gives 2-approx; Christofides 1.5-approx.
• Geometric MST: Euclidean MST on point sets via Delaunay triangulation + Prim.
• Cluster validity / silhouette analysis: MST-based cohesion metrics.
Prim quietly powers many systems where Kruskal would be awkward to plug in.
